Turn 45 ‚ÄĒ Certificate D.1: residual degree-10 Bockstein witness package The problem remains the over-G isomorphism question for the quotients defined by rS,rH, with F=P‚čäG and P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1.The proof sweep has already reached the certificate stage: Turn 44 split the remaining reproducibility work into the linear primitive-boundary certificates and the nonlinear Bockstein/witness certificates, with the first nonlinear block being the residual map ker‚ąā8‚ü∂L10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2].

The new result of this turn is:Certificate¬†D.1¬†is¬†now¬†explicit:¬†ker‚ąā8‚ÜíL5[2]has¬†six¬†displayed¬†independent¬†Bockstein¬†shadows.This supplies an appendix-level witness for the first nonlinear Bockstein step, rather than only quoting the rank-six statement from Turn 7. Turn 7 had established that actual restricted representatives, not naked associative initial forms, induce an onto map ker‚ąā8‚Ü†L10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2],and that this is what kills the first genuine even square obstruction at degree 10.

1. Basis convention

Work in the residual mod-2, s=1, Ef-piece with

X=1+A,Y=1+B,C=(X,Y),E=(X,Y2),and with commutator convention (g,h)=g‚ąí1h‚ąí1gh.Use the following actual restricted group representatives for a basis of L5. I write fi for the degree-5 primitive initial form of the displayed group word:f0f1f2f3f4f5=in5(X,Y4)=ABBBB+BBBBA,=in5(X,C2)=AABAB+AABBA+ABAAB+ABBAA+BAABA+BABAA,=in5 (X,(X,E))=AAABB+AABBA+ABBAA+BBAAA,=in5 (X,(X,(X,C)))=AAAAB+BAAAA,=in5 (X,(Y,E))=AABBB+ABABB+ABBAB+BABBA+BBABA+BBBAA,=in5(Y,C2)=ABABB+ABBAB+BAABB+BABBA+BBAAB+BBABA.

Thus L5[2]is recorded by the square-coordinate basisf0[2],f1[2],f2[2],f3[2],f4[2],f5[2].

The L8 -basis used below is the deterministic ‚Äúactual restricted representative‚ÄĚ basis generated by the Certificate A procedure: squares first, then lexicographic commutators, selecting independent initial forms. This is the same philosophy as Turn 44‚Äôs primitive-basis certificate, except that here the representatives are actual group words so their Bockstein tails are part of the certificate. The history repeatedly warns that replacing these by bare associative initial forms loses the forced pro-2 tails.

2. The degree-10 Bockstein testLet‚ąā8:L8‚äēL8‚ÜíL9,(u,v)‚Ü¶[A,u]+[B,v].

The two entries correspond to corrections placed on Y1 and on the section parameter q. For a kernel vector őļ=(u,v)‚ąąker‚ąā8, choose actual degree-8 representatives U,V, and formőĒőļ=S(Y0(8),Y1(8)U,q(8)V)X‚ąí2 ‚čÖ(S(Y0(8),Y1(8),q(8))X‚ąí2)‚ąí1.

Because őļ‚ąąker‚ąā8, the degree-9 term vanishes:őĒőļ‚ąą1+I10.

The certificate is the projection of in10(őĒőļ) toL10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2].3. Six independent kernel witnesses

Let hi denote the deterministic actual L8 -representatives from the Certificate A selection order. The following six kernel vectors are enough. Each line gives u, v, and its degree-10 Bockstein shadow in L5[2]:witnessőļ13őļ6őļ10őļ1őļ7őļ11uh35h2+h6+h16+h21+h22+h28+h31h3+h6+h8h11h14+h19+h24+h26+h32h19vh5+h30+h33h13+h20h27h0h2+h8+h16+h22h3+h28+h29

Bockstein¬†shadowf0[2]f1[2]f2[2]f3[2]f5[2]f4[2]+f5[2].

These six shadows are independent: the first four give f0[2],f1[2],f2[2],f3[2], the fifth gives f5[2], and the sixth then gives f4[2]after adding the fifth.

Therefore the induced map has image containing the basisf0[2],f1[2],f2[2],f3[2],f4[2],f5[2],so ker‚ąā8‚Ü†L5[2].

4. Why this is the right certificate, not another rank assertion The degree-10 obstruction was the first even layer where the residual quotientL10/im‚ąā9‚ČÖL5[2]was genuinely present. Earlier in the sweep, a fixed gauge displayed a square classe02+e12+e42+e52,but Turn 7 showed that this class is not invariant because degree-8 kernel corrections carry forced Bockstein tails into exactly this six-dimensional quotient.The table above is the missing reproducibility layer: it does not merely state that the induced map has rank 6; it gives six concrete kernel directions and their actual degree-10 shadows. This is the finite witness package needed for the first nonlinear block of Certificate D.

5. What remains in Certificate D

This completes the first nonlinear witness block:D.1¬†residual¬†degree-10:¬†ker‚ąā8‚Ü†L5[2].The remaining nonlinear certificate blocks are now sharply bounded:residual degree 16: the lower-base toggle of the codimension-one őĽ-class;coefficient A2, d=4: the continuation-kernel toggle of őľ4;coefficient d=8: the quotient witnessőł80(0,B8)=(B8)[2],which kills the residual codimension-one miss after passage to H8(2). Turn 44 already identified exactly these as the remaining nonlinear witness blocks.

ESTABLISHED: Certificate D.1 is explicit. Six displayed vectors in ker‚ąā8 have Bockstein shadows spanning L5[2], giving a reproducible witness for the residual degree-10 square-surjectivity ker‚ąā8‚Ü†L5[2].

GAP: The remaining reproducibility gap is now reduced to the three later nonlinear witness blocks: residual degree 16, coefficient A2 at d=4, and coefficient d=8. The structural proof of őďS‚ČÖGőďH is unchanged.

NEXT: Produce Certificate D.2: write the actual residual degree-16 lower-base toggle, including the őĽ-functional, the lower I9 -base seed, and the high-torsor adjustment showing that the codimension-one class is toggled.[TURN45-CERT-D1-DEG10-BOCKSTEIN-WITNESSES]
